name: Divider
description: Section-break band — a single full-bleed block painted with `sys.color.scrimSubtle` (~8% inverse-tone overlay — black scrim in light mode, white scrim in dark) at a fixed block thickness of `sys.layout.stack.xs` (8). The translucent fill stays visible on every host surface tier, so the band reads as a region boundary whether it sits on plain page surface, an elevated container, a hero band, or a coloured card — without retoning per-host. No appearance axis, no thickness prop, no orientation prop: Divider ships one canonical band. The host paints no margin / padding / gap around it — the 8 of scrimSubtle IS the breathing.

aria-hidden.description: Defaults to `true`. The band is decorative chrome — screen readers should not announce it as a thematic break. Override to `false` only when the divider genuinely marks a semantic section change (rare; prefer a heading instead).

className.description: Composes with the component's own class. Use sparingly — Divider exposes no tokens to override and any sizing / colour override breaks the surface-agnostic contract.

container.description: The tonal band itself. `sys.color.scrimSubtle` fill, `sys.layout.stack.xs` (8) block thickness, full inline width, no inline padding, no corner radius, no border. The native `<hr>` element with all browser defaults reset (no margin, no border, no background-image).

appearance.note: The only fill tone — Divider has no emphasis axis. Painted as a translucent ~8% inverse-tone overlay (black in light, white in dark) so the band stays visible on every host surface tier without colliding with a fixed neutral step. Mirrors the `scrimSubtle` contract used by Banner default, Chip / Tag default, Progress track, StatusTag neutral, and Skeleton.

behavior.ariaHidden: Defaults to `aria-hidden="true"` because the band is decorative chrome — screen-reader users navigate by headings and landmarks, not visual breaks.
behavior.fullBleed: Inline width is 100% of the host's content box. Divider claims the page rail in full and must not be wrapped in a padding-inline container — the band reading edge-to-edge IS what makes it a region boundary.
behavior.thicknessFixed: Block thickness is fixed at `sys.layout.stack.xs` (8) and is not a prop. Heavier bands would compete with content; thinner bands collapse to a hairline (use the host's own `outlineVariant` border for row-level breaks, not Divider).

forbidden: divider used as a row separator inside a List — the list's own `divider={true}` paints those as a hairline `outlineVariant` rule
divider painted with `outlineVariant` / a fixed neutral step instead of `sys.color.scrimSubtle` — fixed steps collide with the surface ladder and the divider stops reading on raised surfaces
divider wrapped in a padding-inline div or `style={{ marginInline }}` — Divider is full-bleed and must touch the page edge to read as a region boundary
divider given outer `margin-block` / surrounding `padding-block` to fake more breathing — the 8 thickness is the breathing, and the parent column never pays a gap around Divider
divider painted with a corner radius or stroke — the band is a paint, not a card
divider used inside a Banner / Section header to break up content — that role belongs to body copy structure, not a region break
